objective: Make the frontend Journey Builder node library, backend workflow validation, simulation, and publish/runtime support agree so unsupported nodes cannot be silently published or executed generically.
acceptanceCriteria: Every Journey Builder node type exposed in the frontend is either executed by the backend runtime or blocked before publish with a clear validation error.
Backend capability/validation responses and frontend node availability stay synchronized.
Simulation or dry-run output reflects runtime-supported transitions instead of silently treating unsupported nodes as generic next-step movement.
Focused backend and frontend tests cover the node contract and at least one unsupported-node failure path.
validationEvidence: cd frontend; npm run lint passed
cd frontend; npm run build:ci passed
cd frontend; npx playwright test tests/e2e/automation-builder.spec.ts --project=chromium passed
git diff --check passed with CRLF warnings only
rollbackNotes: Preserve unrelated dirty changes; revert only this checkpoint scope if explicitly approved.
nextAction: Continue with the next highest-priority READY item: tracking-ingress-rate-policy.
